poem.content:
The dust of October settles, a ginger hue,
on a culture built on things we thought we knew.
A critic boils with a silent, slow rage,
judging an entire series, turning a new page.

He teaches his deputy the language of the code,
while out on the water, a story untold.
A thing of the deep, a flash of silver bright,
the tuna runs wild in the fading, pale light.

But beneath the surface, a horror breathes deep,
a blood-red current where secrets sleep.
Enjoy the bounty, the fisherman's cry,
unaware of the shadow passing overhead, high.

The entire coast watches, a silent refrain,
as the boiling waves whisper a long-forgotten name.
October's dust and the ginger's pale fire,
cannot dispel this chilling desire.

It rises, a thing unbidden and vast,
a blood-soaked painting, forever to last.
Deputy watches, wide eyed and still,
life ending quickly, and against your will.
